fields.notes: Manual duration or computed from start/end. Editable for manual entries

fields.name: hourly_rate
fields.notes: Inherited from project or user rate if not set

fields.name: version
fields.notes: Optimistic lock version — incremented on every UPDATE

db_constraints.name: chk_time_entries_deleted_order
db_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

computed.formula: SUM(duration_minutes WHERE is_billable = true AND timesheet_id = timesheets.id)

fields.name: version
fields.notes: Optimistic lock version — incremented on every UPDATE

db_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

fields.name: user_id
fields.notes: null = default rate for all users on project

fields.name: version
fields.notes: Optimistic lock version — incremented on every UPDATE

db_constraints.name: chk_project_rates_deleted_order
db_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

fields.notes: Total seconds accumulated before last pause

fields.name: version

db_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

modules.name: MyTime
modules.description: Personal time tracking: timer, daily log, weekly view

modules.name: RateSettings
modules.description: Configure billing rates per project and user

POST /api/timer/stop.description: Stop timer and create time entry

GET /api/timer/current.description: Get current running timer for user

events.name: timer:started
events.description: Sync timer state across browser tabs

events.name: timer:stopped
events.description: Sync stop event, provides new time entry

flows.name: Start Timer
steps: Check if another timer is already running for user
If yes → prompt to stop or discard existing timer
Create TimerSnapshot with started_at = NOW(), is_active = true
Broadcast timer:started via websocket to sync other tabs

flows.name: Stop Timer
steps: Calculate total duration from accumulated_seconds + (NOW - last_resumed_at)
Convert seconds to minutes (round up to nearest minute)
Navigate to time entry detail for review

flows.name: Submit Timesheet
steps: Validate all entries in period are present and valid
Lock entries from editing (set status = submitted)
Set timesheet status = submitted, submitted_at = NOW()

computed_fields.name: billable_minutes
computed_fields.formula: SUM(time_entries.duration_minutes) WHERE timesheet_id = timesheets.id AND is_billable = true

computed_fields.name: elapsed_display
computed_fields.formula: accumulated_seconds + EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM NOW() - started_at) - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM paused_duration)

domain_rules: Only one active timer allowed per user at a time
Time entries can only be edited when in draft status
Timesheet period is Monday-Sunday (configurable per org)
Timesheets auto-generated weekly; entries assigned by date
Billable rate resolution: entry-level → project/user rate → project default rate → org default rate
Minimum billable duration is 1 minute; sub-minute timers round up
Approved timesheets are locked; require admin to unlock for corrections
Timer state synced across browser tabs via websocket to prevent double-counting
generation_rules: Timer is client-driven with server sync: start/stop written to DB, elapsed time computed client-side
Use localStorage to persist timer state across page refreshes as fallback
Weekly timesheet grid updates optimistically on cell edit, debounce saves 800ms
Duration input accepts HH:MM format and converts to minutes on blur
Billable rate is inherited at time of entry creation and stored; not recalculated on rate change
Timer must survive page navigation: mount TimerWidget at app root level
All time calculations done in UTC; display in user's local timezone

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id

external_entity_refs.name: Project
external_entity_refs.description: Project the time entry is billed against.

external_entity_refs.name: Task
external_entity_refs.description: Task the time entry is linked to.
